claims.claim: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) stops 99.9% of account compromise attacks.
Passwords shorter than 8 characters are considered weak; minimum password length should be 8 characters when MFA is enabled and 15 characters when MFA is not used.
claims.quote: Minimum password length 8 with MFA, 15 without

claims.claim: Password composition rules (such as requiring special characters, uppercase, or numbers) should be avoided in favor of length-based policies.
Breached passwords must be blocked by checking against known breach databases such as the Pwned Passwords API or SecLists.
claims.quote: Block breached passwords via Pwned Passwords API or SecLists

claims.claim: Account lockout should be applied per-account rather than globally, and error messages must be generic to avoid revealing whether an account exists.
claims.quote: Lockout per account. Generic error msg
